1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.
9	// Only actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim; intake_error fail-opens.
10	type PreClaimIntakeOutcome string
11	
12	const (
13		PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_atomic"
14		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
15		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
16		PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"
17		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
18	)
19	
20	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
21	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
22	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
23		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
24		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
25		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`
26		Detail       string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
27		Rewrite      PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
28		// Decomposition is populated when Outcome == too_large_decomposed and the
29		// hook produced a concrete split with children and AC map.
30		Decomposition *PreClaimDecomposition `json:"decomposition,omitempty"`
31	}
32	
33	// PreClaimDecompositionChild is one proposed child bead in a splitter result.
34	type PreClaimDecompositionChild struct {
35		Title       string   `json:"title"`
36		Description string   `json:"description"`
37		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance"`
38		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	// ACMapEntry maps one parent acceptance criterion to child coverage.
42	// Coverage must be non-empty; use "operator_required" or "non_scope" for
43	// ACs that cannot be expressed as a child bead.
44	type ACMapEntry struct {
45		ParentAC string `json:"parent_ac"` // text of the parent AC
46		Coverage string `json:"coverage"`  // child AC text | "operator_required" | "non_scope"
47	}
48	
49	// PreClaimDecomposition is the splitter output when outcome is too_large_decomposed.
50	// Every parent AC must have a corresponding ACMap entry with non-empty Coverage;
51	// a split with missing or empty Coverage entries is lossy and blocks for operator.
52	type PreClaimDecomposition struct {
53		Children  []PreClaimDecompositionChild `json:"children"`
54		ACMap     []ACMapEntry                 `json:"ac_map"`
55		Rationale string                       `json:"rationale"`
56	}
57	
58	// isDecompositionLossy returns true if any ACMapEntry has empty Coverage,
59	// indicating the split does not fully cover all parent acceptance criteria.
60	func isDecompositionLossy(acMap []ACMapEntry) bool {
61		for _, entry := range acMap {
62			if strings.TrimSpace(entry.Coverage) == "" {
63				return true
64			}
65		}
66		return false
67	}
68	
69	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
70	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
71	// claim.
72	type PreClaimIntakeRewrite struct {
73		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
74		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
75		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
76	}
77	
78	// PreClaimIntakeHook classifies a ready bead before Claim. The loop treats
79	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
80	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
81	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)
82	
83	func (r PreClaimIntakeResult) normalizedOutcome() PreClaimIntakeOutcome {
84		switch r.Outcome {
85		case "", PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
86			return PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
87		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
88			PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, PreClaimIntakeError:
89			return r.Outcome
90		default:
91			return PreClaimIntakeError
92		}
93	}

3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead/accheck"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	)
12	
13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead/accheck"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	)
12	
13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}
38	
39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
44		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
45		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
46		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
47	}
48	
49	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
50	type BeadEvent struct {
51		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
52		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
53		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
54		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
55		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
56		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks an explicit external blocker that can be rechecked.
65		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as
66		// blocked.
67		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
68		// StatusProposed marks work awaiting operator approval or clarification.
69		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.
70		StatusProposed = "proposed"
71		// StatusCancelled marks work that will not run and does not satisfy
72		// dependents.
73		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
74	)
75	
76	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
77	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in
78	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
79	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
80	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
81	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
82	// will fail CI.
83	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{
84		StatusOpen,
85		StatusInProgress,
86		StatusClosed,
87		StatusBlocked,
88		StatusProposed,
89		StatusCancelled,
90	}
91	
92	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
93	// in CanonicalStatuses.
94	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
95		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
96			if c == s {
97				return true
98			}
99		}
100		return false
101	}
102	
103	// IssueType constants
104	const (
105		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
106		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
107		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
108	)
109	
110	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
111	const (
112		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
113		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
114		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
115		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
116		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
117		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
118		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
119	)
120	
121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}
145	
146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}
182	
183	// Default values
184	const (
185		DefaultType     = "task"
186		DefaultStatus   = StatusOpen
187		DefaultPriority = 2
188		DefaultPrefix   = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
189		MinPriority     = 0
190		MaxPriority     = 4
191	)
192	
193	// StatusCounts holds aggregate counts for a bead store.
194	type StatusCounts struct {
195		Open              int `json:"open"`
196		InProgress        int `json:"in_progress"`
197		Closed            int `json:"closed"`
198		Blocked           int `json:"blocked"`
199		Proposed          int `json:"proposed"`
200		Cancelled         int `json:"cancelled"`
201		Ready             int `json:"ready"`
202		NeedsHuman        int `json:"needs_human"`
203		WorkerReady       int `json:"worker_ready"`
204		DependencyWaiting int `json:"dependency_waiting"`
205		ExternalBlocked   int `json:"external_blocked"`
206		OperatorAttention int `json:"operator_attention"`
207		Total             int `json:"total"`
208	}
209	
210	// Blocker kinds surfaced through BlockedAll. These strings are part of the
211	// external DDx contract (HELIX reads them to decide how to handle a blocker).
212	const (
213		BlockerKindDependency         = "dependency"
214		BlockerKindBlockedStatus      = "blocked-status"
215		BlockerKindRetryCooldown      = "retry-cooldown"
216		BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation = "needs-investigation"
217		BlockerKindOperatorAttention  = "operator-attention"
218		BlockerKindNotEligible        = "not-execution-eligible"
219		BlockerKindSuperseded         = "superseded"
220		BlockerKindEpicOnly           = "epic-only"
221	)
222	
223	// Blocker describes why an open bead is currently not runnable. Either
224	// unclosed dependencies exist, or an execute-loop cooldown has parked the
225	// bead until NextEligibleAt.
226	type Blocker struct {
227		Kind           string   `json:"kind"`
228		NextEligibleAt string   `json:"next_eligible_at,omitempty"`
229		UnclosedDepIDs []string `json:"unclosed_dep_ids,omitempty"`
230		LastStatus     string   `json:"last_status,omitempty"`
231		LastDetail     string   `json:"last_detail,omitempty"`
232		Reason         string   `json:"reason,omitempty"`
233	}
234	
235	// BlockedBead pairs a bead with its blocker classification.
236	type BlockedBead struct {
237		Bead
238		Blocker Blocker `json:"blocker"`
239	}
240	
241	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
242	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
243		var ids []string
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
246		}
247		return ids
248	}
249	
250	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
251	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			if d.DependsOnID == id {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		return false
258	}
259	
260	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
261	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
262		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
263			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
264				return // already exists
265			}
266		}
267		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
268			IssueID:     b.ID,
269			DependsOnID: depID,
270			Type:        depType,
271			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
272		})
273	}
274	
275	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
276	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
277		var filtered []Dependency
278		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
279			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
280				filtered = append(filtered, d)
281			}
282		}
283		b.Dependencies = filtered
284	}

3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"

9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead/accheck"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	)
12	
13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

5	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
6	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
7	// implement it. Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops,
8	// events, archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and
9	// are composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
10	type RawBackend interface {
11		Init() error
12		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
13		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
14		WithLock(fn func() error) error
15	}
16	
17	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
18	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
19	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
20	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
21	// JSONL export/import.
22	//
23	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
24	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
25	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
26	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
27	//
28	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
29	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
30	type Backend interface {
31		// Foundational
32		Init() error
33		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)

36		Create(b *Bead) error

76	// ResolveTriagePolicy returns the effective triage policy for this config,
77	// layering any `triage.policies` entries on top of the binary default
78	// ladders. Unknown failure-mode names and unknown action names are
79	// silently dropped (schema validation surfaces typos at load time).
80	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveTriagePolicy() triage.TriagePolicy {
81		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
82		if c == nil || c.Triage == nil || len(c.Triage.Policies) == 0 {
83			return policy
84		}
85		for modeStr, actionStrs := range c.Triage.Policies {
86			mode, err := triage.ParseFailureMode(modeStr)
87			if err != nil {
88				continue
89			}
90			ladder := make([]triage.Action, 0, len(actionStrs))
91			for _, a := range actionStrs {
92				act, err := triage.ParseAction(a)
93				if err != nil {
94					continue
95				}
96				ladder = append(ladder, act)
97			}
98			if len(ladder) == 0 {
99				continue
100			}
101			policy.Ladders[mode] = ladder
102		}
103		return policy
104	}

76	// ResolveTriagePolicy returns the effective triage policy for this config,
77	// layering any `triage.policies` entries on top of the binary default
78	// ladders. Unknown failure-mode names and unknown action names are
79	// silently dropped (schema validation surfaces typos at load time).
80	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveTriagePolicy() triage.TriagePolicy {
81		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
82		if c == nil || c.Triage == nil || len(c.Triage.Policies) == 0 {
83			return policy
84		}
85		for modeStr, actionStrs := range c.Triage.Policies {
86			mode, err := triage.ParseFailureMode(modeStr)
87			if err != nil {
88				continue
89			}
90			ladder := make([]triage.Action, 0, len(actionStrs))
91			for _, a := range actionStrs {
92				act, err := triage.ParseAction(a)
93				if err != nil {
94					continue
95				}
96				ladder = append(ladder, act)
97			}
98			if len(ladder) == 0 {
99				continue
100			}
101			policy.Ladders[mode] = ladder
102		}
103		return policy
104	}

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadLint_CLI_PrintsClassification(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	// Create a bead with mixed AC text: 1 test-name (verifiable) + 2 prose.
	out, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Lint test bead",
		"--acceptance", "1. TestFooBar passes\n2. improve clarity\n3. better error messages\n")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	beadID := strings.TrimSpace(out)
	require.NotEmpty(t, beadID)

	// Human-readable output must include kind labels.
	out, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "lint", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "test-name")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "prose")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "bead lint for "+beadID)

	// JSON output must be parseable and include per-AC items.
	out, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "lint", beadID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var result map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &result), "JSON output must be valid: %s", out)
	assert.Equal(t, float64(3), result["total"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), result["verifiable_count"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(2), result["prose_count"])
	items, ok := result["items"].([]any)
	require.True(t, ok, "items must be a JSON array")
	assert.Len(t, items, 3)
}
